description:
A configuration-management system needs to verify that a small Python virtual-environment
    deployment was carried out exactly as requested.  
    Your goal is to create that environment, install two specific packages, and produce a
    text log the CM tool can later parse.

    Target working directory  
    • /home/user/projects/env-demo

    What must exist when you are done
    1. A Python virtual environment located at  
       /home/user/projects/env-demo/venv  
       (it has to be created with the standard “python3 -m venv” mechanism).
    2. Inside that venv, the following two exact package versions must be installed:  
       • requests==2.31.0  
       • PyYAML==5.4.1
    3. A file called /home/user/projects/env-demo/requirements.txt containing **exactly** the
       two lines below, in this order and with a terminating newline after the second line:  
           requests==2.31.0  
           PyYAML==5.4.1
    4. A text log called /home/user/projects/env-demo/setup.log whose contents are **exactly**  
       (including capitalisation, colons, spaces and blank line) the following four lines:  

       TIMESTAMP: 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z  
       VIRTUAL_ENV: venv  
       PACKAGES:  
       requests==2.31.0, PyYAML==5.4.1  

       Notes for the log:
       • No extra spaces before/after any line.  
       • The third line is literally the word “PACKAGES:” (including the colon) and nothing else.  
       • The fourth line lists both packages on the same line, separated by exactly one comma
         and one space.  
       • The file must end with a final newline character.

    Hints
    • Adopt any directory-creation steps that might be required.  
    • Ensure the activate script is used so that `pip` points to the virtual environment.  
    • After installing the packages, double-check package versions with `pip freeze` before
      writing the two output files.

    When the automated grader runs it will check:
    • The venv directory exists and contains the usual sub-directories (bin, lib, etc).  
    • Activating the venv makes “python -m pip show requests” report version 2.31.0, and
      “python -m pip show PyYAML” report version 5.4.1.  
    • requirements.txt and setup.log exist at the specified paths and their contents match
      the exact formats described above, including newlines.
truth:
<!-- Expected artefacts created by the agent -->

    <!-- Directory -->
    /home/user/projects/env-demo/

    <!-- Virtual environment: grader will look for this directory and for
         /home/user/projects/env-demo/venv/bin/activate
    -->
    /home/user/projects/env-demo/venv/

    <!-- requirements.txt exact expected content with trailing newline -->
    File: /home/user/projects/env-demo/requirements.txt
    Content (including terminating newline):
    requests==2.31.0
    PyYAML==5.4.1

    <!-- setup.log exact expected content with trailing newline -->
    File: /home/user/projects/env-demo/setup.log
    Content (including terminating newline):
    TIMESTAMP: 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z
    VIRTUAL_ENV: venv
    PACKAGES:
    requests==2.31.0, PyYAML==5.4.1
